# Seattle Hotel Booking Agent - Implementation Plan

## Problem Statement
Build a .NET 10 Aspire app with an Azure AI Foundry-hosted agent that serves as a Seattle hotel booking assistant. The app uses Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) for orchestration, Microsoft.Extensions.AI (MEAI) for model abstraction, and local C# tools for hotel operations. It should work both locally (Aspire) and deployed to Azure (Foundry/ACI).

## Approach
1. Scaffold the Aspire solution structure (AppHost, ServiceDefaults, Api)
2. Create fake Seattle hotel data and C# tool functions
3. Wire up the agent with MAF + MEAI + Foundry endpoint
4. Add REST API endpoints (search, book, chat)
5. Add Aspire orchestration + deployment support (agent.yaml, Dockerfile, azure.yaml)
6. Provision Azure resources and test end-to-end
7. Write README with setup instructions
8. Write learnings for the future tutorial
9. Write a step-by-step Quick Start tutorial in `./tutorials/` (replaces the existing Foundry hosted agent quickstart)

## Tech Stack
- .NET 10 (SDK 10.0.200+)
- Aspire SDK ~13.2.x
- Microsoft.Agents.AI (MAF) 1.0.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.AI / Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI 10.x
- Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.x
- Azure.Identity (DefaultAzureCredential)
- Azure AI Foundry (gpt-4o or gpt-4o-mini)

## Todos

### Phase 1: Project Scaffolding
- `scaffold-solution` - Create .NET 10 Aspire solution with 3 projects (AppHost, ServiceDefaults, Api)
- `scaffold-apphost` - Configure AppHost to register the API project with external HTTP endpoints
- `scaffold-service-defaults` - Set up OpenTelemetry, health checks, service discovery

### Phase 2: Core Agent Implementation
- `create-hotel-data` - Create fake Seattle hotel data (8-10 hotels with rooms, amenities, prices)
- `create-hotel-tools` - Build C# tool functions: SearchHotels, GetHotelDetails, CheckAvailability, BookRoom
- `create-agent` - Wire up ChatClientAgent with MAF + MEAI + Foundry, register tools via AIFunctionFactory
- `create-endpoints` - Add REST API endpoints: POST /api/chat, GET /api/hotels, GET /api/hotels/{id}

### Phase 3: Configuration & Local Testing
- `add-configuration` - Add appsettings.json with AzureAI section (ProjectEndpoint, ModelDeployment)
- `provision-azure` - Provision Azure AI Foundry resources using az/azd commands
- `test-local` - Run locally with Aspire, test with HTTP requests

### Phase 4: Deployment Support
- `add-deployment` - Add agent.yaml, Dockerfile, azure.yaml for Foundry/ACI deployment
- `test-deployed` - Deploy and test in Azure

### Phase 5: Documentation & Tutorial
- `write-readme` - Write README.md with prerequisites, setup, run, and deploy instructions
- `write-learnings` - Document learnings in ./learnings/ for the future tutorial
- `write-tutorial` - Write a step-by-step Quick Start tutorial in `./tutorials/quickstart-hosted-agent.md`. This is a primary deliverable — a C#-focused replacement for the existing tutorial at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/agents/quickstarts/quickstart-hosted-agent?pivots=azd. The tutorial should walk a developer through the entire process: prerequisites, provisioning Azure resources, creating the project, adding the agent code, testing locally, and deploying. Use learnings captured in `./learnings/` to ensure accuracy and call out gotchas.

## Notes
- Reference projects:
  - ClaimsAgent (Aspire + MAF pattern): https://github.com/JeremyLikness/foundrytest
  - AgentWithTools (hosted agent pattern): generated by `azd ai agent init`
  - GitHub repo for ClaimsAgent: https://github.com/JeremyLikness/foundrytest
- Use DefaultAzureCredential (no API keys)
- The Foundry endpoint needs path stripping for AzureOpenAIClient (host only) vs project endpoint (full path)
- Agent instructions should be hotel-booking-specific: help users find and book hotels in Seattle
